findings.description: Slide 2 (Novo Nordisk at a glance) has a multi-column grid layout with statistics in colored tiles that is collapsed into a single-column linear layout in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows a grid of colored tiles with statistics (50%, 290.4, 80, 52, 45.2) alongside therapy area images in a structured layout. HTML renders all content as a sequential single-column list.

findings.description: Slide 4 (Novo Nordisk Product Supply in NC & Virginia) has a mosaic/grid layout of statistics and images collapsed to single column
findings.evidence: PDF shows a complex grid with tiles containing statistics (31 years, $10B, 60+, LEED Gold, $40M+) and images arranged in a mosaic. HTML renders these as a linear sequence of text items followed by a composite image.

findings.description: Slide 5 (Novo Nordisk in North Carolina) has a two-column layout with images on the left and bullet points on the right, collapsed in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows aerial facility images stacked vertically on the left with bullet points and statistics on the right. HTML places images above the text in a single column.

findings.description: Slide 6 (four must-win battles) has a structured layout with numbered items around a central globe illustration, collapsed in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows #1-#4 positioned around a central globe graphic in a quadrant-like arrangement. HTML renders as a sequential list.

findings.description: Slide 7 (Must-win challenge to reduce CO2 emissions) has a two-column layout with illustration left and targets right, collapsed in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows city/sustainability illustration on left with three targets stacked on right (Net Zero by 2045, 100% renewable by 2030, Zero CO2 by 2030). HTML renders sequentially.

findings.description: Slide 8 (Driving change as environmentally responsible business) has a multi-column layout with three circular elements (Circular supply, company, products) collapsed in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows three columns with circular images and descriptions for supply, company, and products. HTML renders as single column.

findings.description: Slide 10 (Mapping Strategy) has a hierarchical diagram showing Site Usage > Building Usage > Process/Other Usage > M/U, rendered as flat text in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows a structured hierarchical box diagram with external/internal data flows. HTML renders as flat text listing: 'Building Usage | Building Usage | Building Usage' and 'M U | M U | M U'.

findings.description: The mapping strategy diagram (slide 10) is a structured hierarchical chart rendered as unstructured plain text
findings.evidence: PDF shows nested boxes representing Site Usage broken into Building Usage, then Process/Other Usage, then Measured/Unmeasured categories with data flow arrows. HTML shows this as flat text strings separated by pipes.

findings.description: Slide 11 (Goal - Mapping) has a two-column layout with bullet points on the left and a pie chart on the right, collapsed in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows bullet points alongside a pie chart labeled 'Baseline Electricity Consumption'. HTML renders bullets first then pie chart below with the breakdown as a text list.

findings.description: Slide 13 (Water Example - Process Flows) has a two-part layout with drivers on the left and a complex Sankey/flow diagram on the right, collapsed in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows driver bullets alongside a detailed water flow diagram. HTML renders the drivers and all flow values as a long sequential text list.

findings.description: The complex water process flow diagram on slide 13 is not properly reproduced in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows a detailed Sankey-style water flow diagram with color-coded streams, measurement points, and flow values. HTML reduces this to a series of text labels and numbers without the visual flow relationships.

findings.description: Slide 14 (Water Mapping - Visualization) has a two-column layout with heat map and bar chart side by side
findings.evidence: PDF shows a tree map/heat map on the left and a stacked bar chart on the right. HTML shows them stacked vertically.

findings.description: Slide 17 (Energy Savings Project Examples) has a two-column layout with Inactivation Skid on left and Distillation Optimization on right
findings.evidence: PDF shows two project examples side by side in columns. HTML renders them sequentially.

findings.description: Slide 18 (Water Savings Project Examples) has a two-column layout with Filter backwash on left and Conductivity Setpoint on right
findings.evidence: PDF shows two project examples side by side. HTML renders them sequentially.

findings.description: Several visualization images (heat maps, bar charts) on slide 14 are present but very small and hard to read in both versions
findings.evidence: PDF shows dashboard visualizations at a readable size; HTML shows them at a similar or smaller scale but they are present.

findings.description: Slide 3 (Our global presence) has a two-part layout with organizational details on left and a world map with statistics on right, collapsed in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows company details and world map side by side with a pink box showing '80 countries with affiliates'. HTML renders as sequential text with a dark map image.

findings.description: Multiple h1 headings used throughout the HTML document. Nearly every page's main title is rendered as h1, resulting in approximately 15+ h1 elements instead of a single document-level h1.
findings.evidence: The HTML has h1 tags for: 'Energy and Water at Novo Nordisk', 'Novo Nordisk at a glance', 'Our global presence', 'Novo Nordisk Product Supply in North Carolina & Virginia', 'Novo Nordisk in North Carolina', 'We are focusing on four must-win battles', 'Must-win challenge to reduce CO2 emissions', 'Driving change as an environmentally responsible business', 'Circular for Zero – Energy and Water Goals', 'Mapping Strategy – Energy and Water', 'Goal - Mapping', 'Natural Gas and Steam Example', 'Water Example – Process Flows', 'Water Mapping – Visualization', 'Mapping through collaboration', 'Goal – Project Savings', 'Energy Savings Project Examples', 'Water Savings Project Examples', 'Questions?'

findings.description: The pie chart legend for baseline electricity consumption lists 'Est. Conv. Losses' at 6% but the PDF chart shows it at 3%, while 'Production Area 3' shows at 3% but appears to be 6% in the PDF chart.
findings.evidence: HTML lists: '60% Utilities, 9% Production Area 2, 8% Production Area 4, 7% Production Area 1, 7% Administrative Building, 6% Est. Conv. Losses, 3% Production Area 3'. The PDF pie chart shows 3% and 7% at top, with Est. Conv. Losses appearing to correspond to 3% and Production Area 3 to 6%.

findings.description: Minor typo from the PDF preserved in HTML: 'produtciton' instead of 'production' in the water process flow. This is actually faithful to the source, so not an error of conversion.
findings.evidence: HTML: 'GN Utility: Softened water produtciton' — matches PDF source text.

findings.description: The HTML adds '170 countries' as a standalone line that doesn't clearly appear as such in the PDF. The PDF mentions '170' in relation to something else (the number appears alongside affiliates info), but '170 countries' as a standalone claim isn't clearly stated in the source.
findings.evidence:
HTML: '

170 countries

' appears as standalone text. PDF page 2 shows '170' but in context of affiliates/countries the main stat is '80 countries'. The '170' in the PDF appears without clear labeling.

findings.description: The page header 'Novo Nordisk' or 'Novo Nordisk ®' is repeated many times throughout the HTML as div elements, mirroring the PDF's repeated headers/footers on each slide. While this is a faithful reproduction, it creates redundancy.
findings.evidence:
Multiple instances of '
Novo Nordisk ®
' throughout the document.

findings.description: The URL from the PDF footnote is broken into multiple separate anchor tags rather than a single link.
findings.evidence: PDF has one URL: 'https://companiesmarketcap.com/pharmaceuticals/largest-pharmaceutical-companies-by-market-cap/'. HTML splits it into multiple tags: '1. https://companiesmarketcap.com/pharmaceuticals/largest -pharmaceutical -companies...' though all point to the same destination.
